name: Earle Hagen
description: American composer who created music for movies and television (1919-2008)
abstract: Earle Harry Hagen (July 9, 1919 – May 26, 2008) was an American composer who created music for films and television. His best-known TV themes include The Dick Van Dyke Show, I Spy, That Girl and The Mod Squad.

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Hagen and I were co-composers. We split the composing chores right down the middle.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Is Earle still around? ... Oh, so Earle wrote “Harlem Nocturne,” good piece. ... very inventive composer, did a lot of those good things like, “Mod Squad,”
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Urbie Green
about_quotes.evidence: his best friend in that band was Earle Hagen... And he played trombone, and later became one of the big Hollywood writers and composers
about_quotes.doc_title: Urbie Green interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing a minimum of six half hours shows a week with Earle Hagen
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
